findings.evidence: `rg "timeAxis_.*(conj|nonneg|im|re|ofReal)" code/TauCeti/Analysis/PositiveDefinite` only finds the new kernel/sum/predicate/bound/package; by contrast `SemigroupGroupTimeSlice.lean:76-96` exposes `timeSlice_diagonal_nonneg`, `@[simp] timeSlice_diagonal_im`, `timeSlice_diagonal_re_nonneg`, and `timeSlice_diagonal_eq_ofReal_re`.
findings.fix: Add time-axis value lemmas such as `timeAxis_nonneg`, `@[simp] timeAxis_im`, `timeAxis_re_nonneg`, `timeAxis_eq_ofReal_re`, and optionally `@[simp] timeAxis_conj`, proved from `hF.diagonal_nonneg (t / 2)`/kernel symmetry and annotated like the existing slice diagonal API.
findings.issue: The public time-axis API stops at `timeAxis_isPositiveDefinite`/kernel and Cauchy--Schwarz, but does not expose named eliminators that `F (t, 0)` is real and nonnegative for every `t`; downstream Laplace/Bernstein use needs this without rederiving through halves or an unrelated slice module.

summary: The time-axis restriction is exposed, but its characteristic API is missing the real/nonnegative value lemmas needed by the stated BCR Laplace/Bernstein target.
